issue_text:
Alternative to `grid` for style which only draws a line between files
I'm not a fan of having my `cat`-alike draw a full-blown table, but I would like to have some visible "end of file, beginning of next file" separation.

Unfortunately, `--style=header,grid` still draws the horizontal lines as if they were part of a table, resulting in two lines where only one is necessary:

![Spectacle J14905](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/46915/91717467-1989ef00-eb81-11ea-9f23-3664eadc871d.png)

I also get back a full-blown table if I ask for something like line numbers.

What I'd really like is something more minimal than `grid`, which just draws a horizontal line above (or above and below) the `header` to make the change from one file to another more visible when paging through.
issue_title: Alternative to `grid` for style which only draws a line between files
